excerpt: Pay your Council Tax online, by phone, at a Post Office or PayPoint. Pay your Council Tax. See the various ways of paying your Council Tax.

Pay your Council Tax online, by phone, at a Post Office or PayPoint. Pay your Council Tax. See the various ways of paying your Council Tax.

Council Tax instalments, which aren't paid by Direct Debit, are due on or before the 10th of each month. You need to make your payments on time to avoid receiving reminders. You can use your credit or debit card to pay your bill. To pay over the phone by credit or debit card phone our automated payments line with your card details and Council Tax reference number to hand. This service is free. You can pay at any Post Office or PayPoint outlet. You must always present your bill or document with a barcode on it when making your payment. It usually takes up to 3 working days for us to receive your payment. The software we use to allocate payments has built-in rules to determine where payment is posted if you have debts for different years. title: Travel Disruptions Reported Across Rochdale Network
excerpt: Multiple transport services in Rochdale are experiencing delays and disruptions due to ongoing issues and maintenance work.
content_html:
Transport for Greater Manchester (TfGM) has issued alerts regarding disruptions affecting various services in the Rochdale area. These changes are expected to impact travel plans for many commuters and residents.

As of 8 July, delays are reported on the Altrincham and Manchester Airport lines due to a points fault at Market Street. Passengers are advised to plan their journeys accordingly as the situation develops.

Additionally, the lift at Central Park tram stop, which serves the route towards Rochdale via Oldham, is currently out of service. Travellers needing step-free access are directed to use the lift from The Gateway to the Manchester bound platform and then cross the tracks to reach the Oldham bound platform.

Essential improvement works are also scheduled at Prestwich Metrolink stop from 18 May to 19 August. While tram services will not be affected, there may be an increased presence of staff at this location during the works.

Passengers are encouraged to check for updates on the TfGM travel alerts page to stay informed about any further changes or disruptions to services.
legal_disclaimer: This article was compiled from identified public sources and may be updated when further verified information becomes available.

title: Rochdale Council Website Recognised for Accessibility and Quality
excerpt: Rochdale Council's website has achieved top rankings in the Sitemorse UK Local Government INDEX, highlighting its commitment to accessibility and quality.
content_html:
Rochdale Council has announced that its website has received accolades for its accessibility and quality, ranking in the top 10 of the Sitemorse UK Local Government INDEX.

The Sitemorse INDEX evaluates over 400 local government websites across the UK, focusing on performance, compliance, accessibility, and overall quality.

Rochdale's website has been recognised for its commitment to making online services accessible to all residents, reflecting the council's dedication to improving digital engagement.

The council has been awarded certificates for its achievements in this area, showcasing its efforts to enhance user experience and service delivery through its online platform.

title: Getting to Number One Riverside: Your Guide to Accessing Council Services
excerpt: Number One Riverside, the Rochdale Borough Council offices, is easily accessible by public transport and offers nearby parking options.
content_html:
Number One Riverside serves as the main council office for Rochdale Borough Council, located on Smith Street. This multi-use public building is designed to accommodate various council services and community needs.

For those planning to visit, Number One Riverside is conveniently situated just a minute's walk from Rochdale Interchange and the Rochdale Town Centre Bee Network stop. Additionally, Rochdale Train Station is a 14-minute walk away, with tram services available to reach the town centre quickly.

Visitors can find car parking options within a short walking distance from the building. There are also designated spaces for disabled parking, where blue badge holders can benefit from free parking at several council car parks.

The opening times for Number One Riverside align with those of Rochdale Central Library and the Customer Service Centre, ensuring that residents have ample opportunity to access council services throughout the week.

title: Support for Armed Forces Community in Rochdale
excerpt: Rochdale Borough Council offers various support services for current and former armed forces members and their families.
content_html:
Rochdale Borough Council has announced a range of support services for current and former armed forces members and their families. This initiative aims to provide assistance and advice to those who have served in HM Armed Forces.

The Royal British Legion is seeking volunteers to help with fundraising for the Poppy Appeal. This is an opportunity for community members to engage and socialise with fellow veterans over a cooked breakfast or coffee at weekly clubs across Heywood, Middleton, and Rochdale.

In a significant move, the 2021 census included a question regarding military service for the first time, reflecting the importance of recognising the contributions of service personnel.

The council offers guaranteed interviews for ex-service members applying for jobs, provided they meet the necessary criteria. Additionally, armed forces members who are away due to service can apply for a Council Tax discount.

Support is available locally and nationally, ensuring that armed forces members and their families have access to the help they need, regardless of their circumstances.

title: £20 Million Regeneration Initiative Moves Forward in Smallbridge
excerpt: A significant £20 million regeneration initiative aimed at revitalising Smallbridge, Hurstead, and Belfield has made progress with the establishment of a dedicated board.
content_html:
Rochdale Borough Council has announced that a £20 million initiative to boost regeneration across Smallbridge, Hurstead, and Belfield is advancing with the formation of a board and chair to oversee the project.

This initiative aims to enhance the local environment and economy, providing opportunities for residents and businesses in the area.

The council's commitment to regeneration reflects its ongoing efforts to improve community facilities and infrastructure, ensuring a better quality of life for local residents.

Further details about the specific projects and timelines are expected to be released as the board begins its work.

This initiative is part of a broader strategy to foster growth and development within the Rochdale borough, aligning with the council's vision for a thriving community.

title: Rochdale Council Media Enquiry Form Now Available
excerpt: Rochdale Borough Council has launched a new media enquiry form to streamline communication with the public and media.
content_html:
Rochdale Borough Council has introduced a new media enquiry form aimed at improving communication with both the public and media outlets. This initiative is part of the council's ongoing efforts to enhance transparency and accessibility.

The form allows users to submit media enquiries directly to the council's media office. Each page of the form must be completed within 20 minutes, ensuring a quick and efficient process for users.

This update is expected to facilitate better engagement between the council and the community, allowing for timely responses to media-related queries.

Residents and media representatives are encouraged to utilise this new resource to stay informed about council activities and initiatives.

title: Rochdale Council Offers Support Amid Cost of Living Crisis
excerpt: Residents can access various forms of assistance to help manage financial pressures.
content_html:
Rochdale Borough Council has announced a range of support services available to residents facing challenges due to the ongoing cost of living crisis.

The council acknowledges the difficulties many are experiencing and encourages those in need to seek help. Support includes assistance with debt management, employment, heating, and food security.

Residents can access help through various channels, including face-to-face consultations, online resources, and telephone support. The council's lines are open Monday to Friday from 9am to 5pm.

Additionally, Groundwork's Green Doctors are providing free, impartial advice to help residents save energy and reduce their bills, contributing to a more comfortable living environment.

The council also highlights that government assistance is available for both households and businesses, with potential extra payments for those entitled to certain benefits or tax credits.

title: Rochdale Council Updates Bin Collection Information
excerpt: Residents can now check their bin collection days online through the Rochdale Council website.
content_html:
Rochdale residents can easily find out their bin collection days by visiting the Rochdale Borough Council website. The service allows users to enter their postcode and select their address to view specific collection schedules.

Once the postcode is entered, residents can also download and print their bin collection calendar for convenience. This feature aims to help households stay organised and ensure they do not miss collection days.

In case of any issues, such as a postcode not being found, residents are encouraged to report these problems using the contact details provided on the website.

This online service is part of the council's efforts to improve communication and accessibility for residents regarding waste management services.

These services are part of the council's ongoing efforts to enhance public transport options within the borough, making it easier for people to travel for work, education, and leisure activities.

The council encourages locals to take advantage of these new transport options, which aim to provide more accessible routes across the area.

Further details about the specific services and routes can be found on the Rochdale Council website, where residents can stay informed about future updates and improvements.

title: Rochdale Council Offers COVID-19 Support and Guidance
excerpt: Rochdale Borough Council has updated its COVID-19 support services, including vaccination details and mental health resources.
content_html:
Rochdale Borough Council has announced new updates regarding COVID-19 support services available to residents. The council's community response team has been recognised for their collaborative efforts during the pandemic, focusing on problem-solving and skill-sharing.

Residents can find information on how to get tested for COVID-19, details of vaccination clinics, and the latest guidance on keeping safe. The council encourages everyone to stay informed about their vaccination schedules.

In addition to health services, the council is offering support for those facing financial difficulties. Residents can apply for assistance with food, household items, and energy costs, especially during the winter months.

Mental health support is also a priority, with resources available for those experiencing wellbeing issues. The council highlights that one in four individuals may face mental health challenges at some point in their lives, and encourages seeking help when needed.

For more information on these services, residents can visit the Rochdale Council website, where they can access advice and links to relevant organisations.

title: Rochdale Council Provides Emergency Update Information
excerpt: Rochdale Borough Council has outlined how residents can stay informed during emergencies and adverse weather conditions.
content_html:
Rochdale Borough Council has issued guidance on how residents can stay updated during emergencies or adverse weather conditions. Regular updates will be provided to local media and through the council's website.

Residents are encouraged to tune into local broadcast media for timely news. The council has listed various local and regional news sources, including radio stations and newspapers, where updates can be accessed.

In addition to traditional media, residents can follow key organisations on social media for real-time information during emergencies. This approach aims to ensure that the community remains informed and prepared.

The council's commitment to keeping the public informed highlights the importance of communication during critical situations, allowing residents to take necessary precautions.

title: Rochdale Council Promotes Summer Activities and Green Spaces
excerpt: Rochdale Council highlights summer activities, including free bike loans and award-winning parks, encouraging residents to engage with local leisure options.
content_html:
Rochdale Council has announced a range of summer activities aimed at encouraging residents to get active and enjoy the borough's green spaces.

At Bowlee Garden Nursery, residents can purchase summer bedding plants, planters, and hanging baskets to enhance their gardens. Additionally, the borough's bike library offers free bike loans, making cycling accessible to all.

The council emphasises that exercise is for everyone, not just fitness enthusiasts, and promotes various ways for residents to engage in regular physical activity.

Many parks in the Rochdale borough have received the prestigious Green Flag Award, recognising them as some of the best-managed parks in the UK. This accolade highlights the quality of green spaces available for public enjoyment.

Residents are encouraged to explore local cultural venues, attractions, and festivals, which showcase the rich history and stunning scenery of Rochdale. With over 900 years of history, there is much to discover in the area.

title: Rochdale Council Enhances Transport and Parking Services
excerpt: Rochdale Council is improving transport and parking facilities across the borough, focusing on safety and accessibility.
content_html:
Rochdale Council has announced several initiatives aimed at enhancing transport and parking services throughout the borough. The updates include the development of a fully-integrated transport system that promotes walking and cycling, particularly through the Castleton scheme, which aims to create a safer environment for residents to exercise.

Residents can now access information about council car parks, including those with Blue Badge parking spaces and electric car charging points. This initiative is part of the council's broader effort to make the borough more accessible for all users.

The council's Highways Team has recently received a Gold Award for Street Data, recognising their collaboration with local government departments and utilities to improve planning and execution of roadworks.

In addition to parking improvements, the council is also focused on public safety during adverse weather conditions. They provide information on gritting routes and advice on how to stay safe during cold weather, as well as guidance on reporting flooding and what actions to take before, during, and after such events.

Residents are encouraged to pay parking fines and moving traffic camera fines securely online, streamlining the process and enhancing convenience for users. The council continues to work diligently to improve the overall experience for residents, visitors, and businesses in Rochdale.

The updates aim to keep the community informed about various happenings and initiatives that may affect local residents.

Residents are encouraged to view the updates regularly to stay engaged with local governance and community activities.

title: Rochdale Council Updates Bin Collection Services
excerpt: Rochdale Council has provided important updates regarding bin collection services, including changes due to weather and recycling guidelines.
content_html:
Rochdale Council has announced updates regarding its bin collection services, which are essential for residents and businesses in the borough.

Changes to bin collections may occur during severe weather conditions, potentially causing delays. Residents are advised to stay informed about collection schedules during such times.

For businesses employing ten or more people, separate bins and collections for different types of waste are required. This is part of the council's efforts to promote effective waste management.

The council also offers a bulky waste collection service for unwanted household items, including beds, mattresses, fridges, and furniture, making it easier for residents to dispose of large items responsibly.

Residents are encouraged to recycle correctly by understanding what items can be placed in each bin. The council provides resources to help residents make the most of their waste, including tips on reducing food waste through the Love Food, Hate Waste initiative.

title: Rochdale Council Offers Online Access to Council Tax Services
excerpt: Residents can now manage their Council Tax accounts online through Rochdale Council's MyAccount service, aimed at easing financial pressures.
content_html:
Rochdale Borough Council has announced that residents can now access their Council Tax accounts online through the MyAccount service. This initiative is designed to provide a quicker and more efficient way for residents to manage their accounts and benefit services.

The MyAccount service allows users to view their Council Tax bills, make payments, and request new copies of bills if needed. This move is part of the council's efforts to reduce paper usage and improve environmental sustainability.

In light of the ongoing cost of living crisis, the council is encouraging residents to explore available Council Tax discounts. These discounts may apply to individuals such as those with disabilities, single occupants, and care leavers.

Residents can find their 8-digit Council Tax reference number on their bills, which is necessary for accessing the online services. The council has highlighted that using MyAccount is free and provides a safer way to manage personal information.

For those moving to or from the borough, it is important to inform the council for accurate Council Tax purposes. More information on these services can be found on the Rochdale Council website.

title: Kirkholt Man Jailed for Car Theft Following High-Speed Chase
excerpt: A 23-year-old man from Kirkholt has been sentenced to 30 months in prison after pleading guilty to burglary and vehicle theft. The incident involved a high-speed police pursuit and substantial evidence linking him to the crime.
content_html:
A man from Kirkholt has been sentenced to 30 months in prison after pleading guilty to burglary and taking a vehicle without the owner's consent.

an individual, 23, was involved in the theft of a BMW from a property in Warrington, Cheshire, on February 6. He and an accomplice broke into the house and stole the car keys before driving off.

The vehicle was tracked down by police just 35 minutes after the theft, following a report from the homeowner who captured the incident on their mobile phone.

During the pursuit, the BMW was driven at speeds exceeding 100mph before crashing into a parked car and a wall. Both suspects fled the scene but were apprehended shortly after.

Police secured a significant amount of evidence, including a footwear mark found at the scene that matched the shoes Lamb was wearing, as well as mobile phone data linking him to the crime. Lamb was sentenced an individual Court on July 3.

content_html:
Your Trust Rochdale has announced new health and fitness membership options aimed at encouraging residents to lead active, creative, and healthy lives. As a charity, they focus on supporting the local community through various fitness initiatives.

Membership options include a 'Swim Only' plan for those interested solely in swimming, as well as 'Plus' and 'Premier' memberships that offer access to group exercise classes, sauna, and steam room facilities.

For those on means-tested benefits, an 'Extra' membership is available, providing a discounted monthly price. This initiative aims to make fitness more accessible to all members of the community.

Members can enjoy access to gyms, swimming pools, and over 160 Les Mills classes on demand through the Your Trust app. The venues include Heywood Sports Village, Littleborough Sports Centre, Middleton Arena, and Rochdale Leisure Centre.

The memberships are structured with a commitment to a minimum of one month for direct debit payments, ensuring that the funds are reinvested into community services. This approach highlights the charity's commitment to supporting local health and fitness initiatives.

excerpt: FirstWestYorks Service 542 is diverting due to ongoing disruption on Gibraltar Road, affecting local bus routes.
content_html:
A traffic diversion is currently in place on Gibraltar Road due to ongoing disruption. The FirstWestYorks Service 542 is being rerouted as a result.

Buses are now diverting via Hopwood Lane, Warley Road, and Spring Hall Lane in both directions. This change is expected to affect local commuters and residents.

an individual confirmed the sentencing, which underscores the ongoing challenges related to vehicle theft in the area. The case has drawn attention to the need for increased vigilance among vehicle owners.
